hints.text: Plates fairly long, most commonly white; **private = red, public = black**
ALL road coverage = **shitcam** (brownish, big circular blur)

shared_with.note: Shitcam is very common in India too.
shared_with.diff: India also has smallcam Gen4 + trekkers; Nepali Devanagari uses **ङ/ङ्ग** and native Devanagari numerals (rare in India).

hints.text: Drives on the **left**
**Devanagari** (top line, straight verticals) — Nepali tells: **ङ / ङ्ग** much more common than in Hindi + native **Devanagari numerals** on signs (very rare in India)

shared_with.diff: ङ्ग + Devanagari numerals + all-shitcam + Himalaya → Nepal.

hints.text: Square concrete poles (flats) / thin metal poles (mountains), often trident tops
bullets: Concrete pole w/ **trident top + triangle supports** = unique to Nepal
Square pole w/ **2 vertical side insulators, wide gap** = almost unique
Other poles can look very Indian
hints.uniq_note: The trident+triangle-support pole is unique, the wide-gap insulator pole almost unique — all other Nepali poles look like India's, so anchor on those two.

shared_with.note: India's most common pole is also square concrete with a trident poletop, without the triangle supports.
shared_with.diff: Triangle supports under the trident, or the wide-gap insulator pole → Nepal; plain trident → could be either, check other clues.

hints.text: **Outer yellow lines** (dashed/solid) + dashed white middle on bigger roads; solid outer yellow ↔ very common in eastern Bagmati
**Black-white striped concrete bollards** (3–5 stripes), often a red top marking
Flat agriculture in the **south**, high-altitude mountain roads in the **northern half**
Colourful blocky multi-storey buildings or small painted-concrete/brick/thatch houses; **triangle-shaped facade decorations** common
**Black-white striped signposts**
White **Buddhist stupas, sometimes with painted eyes** — only in Nepal
**Districts** appear in addresses, often in English too; far south = flat (Terai), centre/north = high mountains

**Vegetation metas**: western hills dry w/ exposed hillsides (east greener); areca palms → southeast; canola → southern pockets in the west; chir pines → mountains (esp. west + near Kathmandu); white fluffy grass → SE of Kathmandu; Japanese cedars + tea plantations → far east only

**Roof metas**: rounded tiles → flats S of Kathmandu + west; West-Bengal-style tiles (diamond protrusions) → far-west flats; grey square stone tiles → Birgunj/Janakpur (NOT in India-lookalike areas!); messy rectangular tiles → western mountain towns; corrugated metal → Birgunj–Janakpur; roof **railings** → west (Tulsipur valley, stair-shaped; east limit ≈ Kawasoti); golden god-carved gables → around Kawasoti; stick fences → eastern plains; triangular roof 'window' + sloping section → far east; hip roofs w/ small ridge protrusion → (west of) Biratnagar
**Mosques** only in Muslim pockets: around Janakpur, Birgunj, Nepalgunj, Biratnagar/W of Koshi; round clay huts w/ thatch → around Birgunj (also across the Indian border)
Poles w/ a **cross near the top** → east; Kathmandu poles: asymmetric crossbar + triangle attachment + support bar; **yellow water tanks** → east (green/black everywhere)

**Car metas**: orange car → Nepalgunj/Madi/Biratnagar/Birtamod (w/ blue ribbon = Biratnagar); blue car → Pokhara/Birgunj/Malangawa (w/ antenna = Pokhara); white car → Butwal, H10, H04, near Kathmandu, NE-Birgunj, Janakpur–Lahan, Itahari; black spoiler → Janakpur; grey car → Kathmandu + Lahan; blocky grey truck → E of Birgunj + S of Kathmandu; grey car w/ backlight → Pokhara/Kathmandu/N-Malangawa/E-Itahari; red car (long antenna) → Kathmandu + N of Janakpur

**Road spotlights**: N of Siddhicharan (east) — high hills full of dark **rhododendron bushes** (red flowers); NW of Bhimeshwor — foggy gloomy road in the only natural **east-Himalayan-fir forest**; NE of Phungling — sketchy yellow/orange dirt road w/ high-elevation oaks; N of Khaptad NP — NW-bound valley dirt track w/ snowcapped mountains NW
**Trekker coverage** only SW of Mount Everest — green forests in the south, barren trails north; near Namche Bazar: sunny near the treeline; compass mostly unreliable!

**Trekker settlements**: Namche Bazar = biggest, dense (+bird's-eye views); Lukla = southernmost, narrow street, extremely dark; Khumjung = jagged peak above + nearly all **green roofs** (label further north is misleading); Pangboche = bright, forested mountains; Phorste = sparse, huge mountain, dead-looking trees
**Trail metas**
bullets: Lukla→Namche: dark overcast forest valley → brighter N of Phakding → sunny Monjo→Namche
Namche→Thame: forested NW-SE valley (Thamu); shining snowy range at Thame
Namche→Gokyo: semi-green river valley in clouds → Tobuche cloud-blanket → smoke-like spot clouds → clear Tobuche → icy lake at Gokyo → barren NNW trail

Gokyo→Lobuche: grey dried **glacial valley** → red rugged mountainside → rising U-valley → spiky range → the country's biggest snow patch (glacier crossing) → irregular clouds → Tobuche from the east → wide high river valley
Namche→Dingboche: overcast mountainside trail high above the valley → bright sandy overcast forest E of Deboche → hazy sun between Pangboche & Dingboche
→ Everest: low-cloud valley N of Dingboche → barren NNE trail → the **view of Mount Everest** at the end
